findings.title: New bulk-recheck 422s on no-match; sibling bulk ops silently 200
findings.why_it_matters: HistoryAPI.bulk_recheck (new in this diff) explicitly treats 'ids provided but none matched a real record' as a hard failure -> the route raises 422 problem+json. Every sibling bulk endpoint in the exact same PR/plan -- DraftAPI.bulk_publish_now, DraftAPI.bulk_cancel, and the pre-existing HistoryAPI.bulk_delete -- silently no-ops the same scenario and returns 200 with a refreshed list and a '0 items' message. A generic bulk-operation consumer (or a future SPA feature that reuses one error-handling code path for all four 'select rows, click bulk action' buttons) cannot assume uniform behavior for the same edge case across this family of otherwise-parallel endpoints. The docstring added for bulk_recheck even flags the sibling behavior as a 'fake 200' it deliberately avoids, showing the author was aware of the inconsistency but only fixed it in one of the four bulk endpoints.
findings.suggested_fix: Decide one intended semantic for 'ids array non-empty but zero records matched' across the whole bulk-ops family (either all four return 422, or all four return a soft 200 with a 0-count message), then align bulk_publish_now/bulk_cancel/bulk_delete with bulk_recheck's new behavior (or revert bulk_recheck to match them) and document the chosen policy once in a shared docstring/spec description rather than per-endpoint.
evidence: webui_app/api/history_api.py:207-213 -- bulk_recheck: `items = self._all_items_by_id(ids)` then `if not items: return {"ok": False, "flash_msg": "未匹配到记录"}` -> routed to 422 by webui_app/api/v1/history.py's `if not result.get('ok'): raise ApiProblem(422, ...)`.

webui_app/api/drafts_api.py:320-334 -- bulk_publish_now: loops ids, `if not item: continue` (silently skips unmatched ids, no ok:False path for zero-match), then unconditionally `return {"ok": True, "flash_msg": f"正在批量发布 {len(completed_jobs)} 项..."}` even when completed_jobs is empty.
webui_app/api/drafts_api.py:381-411 -- bulk_cancel: same silent-skip pattern (`if not item or item.get('status') != 'scheduled': continue`), unconditional ok:True return.

webui_app/api/history_api.py:132-138 -- pre-existing bulk_delete: `removed = bulk_delete_from_db(ids)` then unconditional `return {"ok": True, "flash_msg": f"已删除 {removed} 条历史记录"}` regardless of whether removed is 0.

webui_app/api/v1/history.py:88-104 -- new history_bulk_recheck route docstring explicitly states 'HistoryAPI.bulk_recheck only returns ok: False for genuine input problems (empty/unmatched ids), which is honestly surfaced as 422 rather than a fake 200' -- acknowledging the sibling pattern as a 'fake 200' without changing it.

residual_risks: The OpenAPI diff to openapi/backlink-api.yaml was manually re-verified line-by-line (git diff -U10 763d0280 HEAD): all changed hunks in that file are pure additions (95 insertions, 0 deletions per git diff --stat; no '-' lines touch any existing path/schema block). This corroborates the 'purely additive' claim, but oasdiff itself could not be executed in this environment (Docker unavailable), so the actual CI breaking-change gate has not run against this diff -- structural nuances oasdiff checks (e.g. requestBody/response media-type or nullability changes) are not independently confirmed beyond this manual read.

POST /api/v1/drafts/bulk-publish-now's 409 'already_running' single-flight guard is a module-level `threading.Lock()` (webui_app/api/v1/drafts.py:35), which only provides the documented single-flight contract within one process. The app currently runs via `app.run(...)` (webui.py:113), i.e. single-process, so the guarantee holds today; if the deployment model ever changes to a multi-process WSGI server (e.g. gunicorn with >1 worker), the 409 exclusivity promise in the OpenAPI doc ('A bulk publish is already in progress') would silently stop being enforced across workers with no test to catch the regression.

The 409 payload for bulk-publish-now does carry a distinguishing `error_class: 'already_running'` field (same generic ProblemDetails schema used API-wide), and frontend/src/api/drafts.ts's comment documents this, but no actual frontend code path (DraftsPage.vue's run()/classifyError) branches on it -- classifyError buckets 409 into its generic 'unknown' category identically to any other unexpected error. In the rare cross-tab/multi-session race the comment anticipates, the user sees the same generic '出错了/发生未知错误，请重试' toast as a real failure, even though nothing is actually wrong. This matches the app's existing fixed-copy error-taxonomy convention (not a new pattern), so it is not flagged as a defect, but the comment's framing ('to avoid surfacing that as a user-facing error unnecessarily') slightly overstates what the code actually does for the non-same-tab case.
testing_gaps: tests/test_webui_api_v1_bulk_ops.py covers the drafts/bulk-publish-now 409 (double-submit) and 502 paths, and DraftsPage.spec.ts has a matching UI-level test asserting a 409 rejection surfaces a generic error toast without crashing. History's bulk-recheck has no equivalent UI-level (HistoryPage.spec.ts) test for its new 422 'no items matched' failure path -- only the backend contract test (test_webui_history_bulk_recheck_no_match_is_ok_false_but_422) exercises it, so a regression in how the SPA surfaces that specific 422 would not be caught by the frontend suite.

No test exercises the drafts bulk-publish-now / bulk-cancel 'ids provided but zero match a real record' scenario at all (unlike history's bulk-recheck, which has an explicit test for it) -- so the silent-200-with-0-count behavior called out in the finding above is asserted nowhere and could regress or be 'fixed' inconsistently without any test noticing.

No test asserts on the actual generated openapi/backlink-api.yaml being run through oasdiff (or an equivalent breaking-change linter) in this environment -- the additive-diff claim is manually verified here but the CI gate itself has not executed against this branch.
